title: Running vs Walking
shortAnswer: Running burns twice as many calories per minute as walking but both provide similar cardiovascular benefits. Walking requires longer duration to match running's benefits.

keyDifferences.label: Calorie burn rate
keyDifferences.entityAValue: Burns twice as many calories per minute

keyDifferences.label: Time requirement
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Longer duration required for same benefits

keyDifferences.label: Health benefits
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Equal diabetes, cholesterol, blood pressure benefits

prosA: Burns twice as many calories per minute
prosB: Low impact on joints and bones
Equal health benefits for diabetes and blood pressure
consB: Requires longer duration for same calorie burn
verdict: Both exercises offer excellent health benefits, with walking providing equal protection against diabetes, high cholesterol, and high blood pressure. Choose running for faster calorie burn and time efficiency, or walking for lower impact and sustainability.
